Rmv 1 of 1001 categories, Florida at least is a stretch. Trump was confirmed Presbyterian but afterwards attended a Reformed church with his parents and was married there. His next marriage was performed by a Reformed minister at Trump's Plaza Hotel, and his third marriage took place at an Episcopal church; he visited St. John's, another Episcopal church, for a fairly (in)famous Bible waiving photo op, and in 2020, he said he was a nondenominational Christian.

/* Relationship with the press */ reminder that this is trump's personal article and wikipedia policy requires that it be kept to summary level detail, particularly when further information is available in specific articles. most editors in the talk page agreed that this section needs to be trimmed significantly.

/* Relationship with the press */ a private company making a private decision is absolutely not due for this article. the rest of the section is about the press in general and reliable sources do not place more emphasis on CBS than every single other media company combined.

/* Social media */ completely UNDUE. mentioning few specific videos posted by his social media account do not warrant inclusion in this article, when the entire rest of the section is about his general social media habits and history. reliable sources do not place anywhere near the same emphasis on the AI videos as they do the bans by various platforms and his falsehoods and misinformation that this section also talks about.

/* False or misleading statements */ undue for this page. the once source that's cited is talking about other stuff for a majority of the aritcle. it is not comparable (even though the current sentence tries to make a comparison in wikivoice) to antimalarial drug falsehoods that are specifically and widely cited by sources.

/* Link to violence and hate crimes */ violation of WP:OR. splicing together one source saying he encouraged an attack against hecklers, and another source on how trump endorsed a congressman who attacked a reporter, does not back this sentence's claim of a general trend of encouraging violence against protestors or reporters

/* Racial and gender views */ more WP:NOTNEWS. every single source that is cited was published the same week. meanwhile, if you search for reliable source coverage in the weeks following, few place significant emphasis on this event. it also is only tangentially related to "racial and gender views" because none of the sources say that clearing the protest had anything to do with racial views.

/* Racial and gender views */ WP:NOTNEWS. the sources on the birther conspiracy span years, while the reporting on Trump's claims about Harris are mostly from the few weeks after he made the claim. clearly not DUE in 2025.

/* Racial and gender views */ absolutely not DUE for a BLP. is an opinion poll on what americans think equivalent to experts analysis of whether Trump is racist or not? and again, why are we mentioning how voters were more likely to back trump because they believed X or Y? you could write an entire article mentioning every factor that led voters to back Trump, but it doesn't belong in his personal article because that's solely about the 2016 and 2020 elections.

/* Political practice and rhetoric */ this is a trivial quote that's barely mentioned by reliable sources. google "trump" "real power" "fear". almost none of the reliable sources place significant emphasis on it, they just mention it as a factoid when discussing a book written by woodward.

/* Political practice and rhetoric */ deleted another BLP violation. this sentence cites two sources to effectively claim in Wikivoice that Trump is a christian nationalist. neither source says this; one source is a research paper with only nine citations that just says christian nationalists were likely to vote for trump, while the other cites anecdotes to say the same thing by interviewing evangelicals. we aren't mentioning every other group that likely voted for trump, so why cherrypick this?

/* Political practice and rhetoric */ this doesn't belong in trump's personal article. it isn't a claim about trump, it's a claim about his 2016 and 2020 campaigns and how the alt-right supported him more than his opponents. few reliable sources claim that this had a significant effect on either of his campaigns, so it doesn't even belong in the campaign sections of this article either.

/* Political practice and rhetoric */ two sources for two serious allegations is absolutely not sufficient in the most viewed BLP on wikipedia. basically accusing trump of being racist and his supporters of being racist because two academics said so. there is an entire racial and gender views section that mentions how Trump has been accused of racist comments, and that is sufficient and actually has context.
/* Foreign policy */ "hardline" is unsourced and untrue.

/* Link to violence and hate crimes */ deleting BLP violation as explained in my previous edit (the source doesn't make this claim, almost no sources mention this niche topic, and it's basically calling trump a terrorist in Wikivoice). an editor reverted me without explanation, but BLP violation's don't get to remain in the article, so I'm deleting it again. please explain on talk why this content should stay.
